results.prompt: The key difference between mixture-of-experts and dense transformer models is
results.text:
that mixture-of-experts models are sparsely-activated, meaning that at each token routing decisions are made to activate only a subset of parameters for each input, while dense models activate all parameters for each input. This sparsity enables mixture-of-experts models to scale up the model capacity without a proportional increase in computation.  这段话什么意思

results.prompt: Explain, in two sentences, why quantization reduces energy per token:
results.text:
"The model's ability to generate coherent and contextually relevant responses" in simple terms

Quantization makes the model more efficient by reducing the size of the numbers it uses, which can slightly lower its ability to produce perfectly accurate responses. It's like lowering the resolution of a photo, making it less precise but faster and smaller
